meta_title: For Founders: SaaS pricing to Solve tutorial hell (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome tutorial hell. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: SaaS Pricing to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Break free from tutorial hell. Learn how to implement SaaS pricing strategies using Antigravity and Claude Code to build profitable, market-ready products.
content.answer_block: Tutorial hell ends when you stop building features and start building businesses. SaaS pricing is the ultimate validator; if users won't pay, your code doesn't matter. By integrating automated billing early with Antigravity and streamlining development via Claude Code, you shift focus from syntax to revenue, turning your MVP into a viable asset.
content.intro:
You’ve spent months watching videos, tweaking boilerplate, and refining UI components that nobody uses. You are trapped in a loop of infinite preparation, chasing technical perfection while your real-world product remains a phantom. The code is clean, but the bank account is empty, and the market doesn't even know you exist.

It is time to stop the cycle. Real SaaS development isn't about the perfect architecture; it’s about the perfect feedback loop. We are going to replace your fear of shipping with the cold, hard reality of a Stripe integration, using modern AI tooling to bridge the gap between 'Hello World' and your first paid subscription.

steps.title: Define Your Value Metric
steps.description: Don't guess your price. Identify the specific unit of value your user pays for—whether it's per seat, per credit, or per project—and codify it.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the core value proposition of a productivity SaaS. Suggest three tiered pricing models based on value metrics like usage volume, team size, and feature gating. Provide a JSON schema for a subscription model.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a pricing.ts interface that defines tiers for Basic, Pro, and Enterprise based on usage limits.'

steps.title: Architect the Billing Flow
steps.description: Integration is where most founders stall. We will use automation to scaffold the Stripe checkout session handlers before writing a single line of business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure Express.js route handler for Stripe checkout sessions. Include logic for customer creation, metadata tagging, and success URL redirection.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate the stripe-webhook.ts file with error handling and database user-sync logic.'

steps.title: Implement Feature Gating
steps.description: Protect your premium features with middleware that checks subscription status, ensuring only paying users access high-value endpoints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript middleware function for a Next.js API that validates user subscription status against a database record before allowing access to restricted endpoints.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Inject the subscription-guard middleware into the /api/dashboard routes.'

steps.title: Automate Subscription Lifecycle
steps.description: Handling cancellations and churn is part of the business. Set up automated webhook listeners to update your database status in real-time.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a robust webhook listener implementation for Stripe events: customer.subscription.deleted and customer.subscription.updated. Ensure local DB sync.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Update webhook handler to sync subscription status and log churn events to the database.'

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Ship the pricing page and the payment flow immediately. Your goal is to get a 'Subscription Created' log, not a perfect UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a minimal, high-conversion pricing component in React/Tailwind that displays three tiers and a clear call-to-action button.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Build the PricingPage.tsx component and link the checkout buttons to the stripe-checkout route.'

pro_tips: Always price based on the value delivered, not the cost of your server resources.
Ship your checkout page before you finish your product dashboard; revenue is the ultimate feature.
Use Stripe’s Test Mode to trigger every failure state, including expired cards and failed webhooks.
Never build custom billing logic; always leverage a provider like Stripe or LemonSqueezy to handle tax and compliance.
If you don't have a 'cancel' button that works, you aren't running a SaaS, you're running a trap.
content.next_steps: Now that the payment infrastructure is live, push your code to production and manually sign up for your own service to test the end-to-end flow. Once confirmed, start reaching out to your first 10 potential users for feedback.
content.conversion_block: You have the tools, the roadmap, and the strategy to stop being a student and start being a founder. Stop watching and start shipping. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
